original: He points one of his yellow-clawed fingers at the bow slung over your shoulder. You blink in surprise - you’d expect to get compliments on a cool new-tech gun or a plasma sword or something, but a simple bow? You’re only a few thousand years out of date there. Still, you can’t help but grin at the comment, out of left field as it is.

“Can’t beat the classics, right? I used to do a lot of bowhunting back on Leitha, myself. One of the reasons I volunteered to come out here, too: plenty of wild game, and a good recurve bow puts me right on level with the natives. Makes it a fair fight when they try to get a little frisky.” He laughs and clatters up onto his long, clawed feet, giving you a first-hand look at his impressive height: he’s nearly ten feet tall, a gray-skinned humanoid from the waist up sat atop a reptilian lower body on six powerful legs. A reptilian tail coils behind him, dexterously avoiding the shelves of hunting trophies behind the desk.
“Big game bowhunter, huh?” you ask, eyeing the trophies behind him.
He grins, extending a hand to you. “Pretty much. Name’s Tanisaran, by the way. Tanisaran Alhelvan. Tanis for short.”

You shake it, noticing his grip is surprisingly gentle - though that may just be him trying not to score your hand with his sharpened claws. “Anyway! You probably wanted to rent a shuttle, right? Something tells me you didn’t just come around to gab about bows.”
Next
“Hey, [pc.name],” Tanis grins as you start to ask about a training session. “Hold that thought a second - I got something for you.”

You follow the big leithan with your eyes as he hops up onto his hexapedal legs and goes around to a small box leaning against the wall. He hefts it up and hands it to you: the box is fairly narrow but very long, three or four feet at least, but feather-light despite its size. “Check it out,” he urges, lizard tail swishing quickly behind him.

You open it, tearing away a string and popping the top off. Inside the box, nestled amongst a bed of packing peanuts and shredded paper, is a compound bow made of the same alien wood and of the same tremendous length that Tanis has been using during your training sessions. With wide eyes, you pick the bow up and heft it in your hands - it weighs next to nothing, and an experimental draw makes the heavy string feel like pulling air thanks to the network of wheels and pulleys supporting your motions.
“It’s awesome,” you breathe, aiming down the shaft of an imaginary arrow.
Tanis grins big. <i>“Glad you like it. It’s yours - if you want it. I figured an expert archer like you deserves a bow worthy of the title. This one’s straight from my family back on Leitha.
It, uh, might be a little big for you, but with the wheels on it you shouldn’t have any trouble with the draw.
I’d almost say it’s a little small for you, but that just means you can pull the string harder and faster, right?
That’s an incredibly generous gift. You
give Tanis a curt nod of thanks
thoroughly thank your patient trainer
, and sling the bow over your shoulder.
Tanis steps forward and plants a hand on your shoulder. “Put it to good use, alright? I want to hear some kick-ass stories some day, alright?”
You can probably manage that!
<i>“Hey,
big game hunter
man

,” you say, stepping over to Tanis’s desk. As you walk, you pull the bow off from over your shoulder and plant the butt of it on the ground beside you. His eyes instantly light up at the sight of it, and his ashen lips twist into a hint of a smile.
“‘Sup,” he answers, eyes wandering over your bow. “Gotta say, if you’re trying to hold up the place, you could have picked a weapon that is a little less conspicuous.”
You chuckle and counter, “Actually, I was wondering if you’ve got some pointers for me. Haven’t exactly had a lot of training using something like this before.”
He smiles and rises to his feet, six bestial legs stretching out from under his centaur-like body. <i>“Sure, I guess I could show you the basics. Not much else to do
with the nav beacons down anyway
ever since they shipped those new robot drivers out here

He fishes around beneath his desk for a moment, pulling a towering bow out from under it. It’s not quite like your bow: bigger, though proportional to his size, and made of a material you can’t quite place. Some alien wood, you’d imagine, though the way he hefts it around makes you think it’s much heavier than any bow you’ve ever seen before. He waves you over around the counter to a small back door that leads you out to the edge of the Mhen’gan jungle.

“So, no formal training, right?” Tanis says, less a question and more an amused statement of fact. When you nod, he chuckles. “Don’t worry about it. Most people don’t. Bowmanship’s kind of a lost art these days, you know. Some of us leithans keep up the old art: nowhere in the galaxy has game like Leitha, though you have to be ready to be prey as much as predator on our homeworld. A strong, silent shot will get you further than the big bang of a rifle when there’s monsters the size of houses eager to eat you. If you can bring down a kill without drawing unwanted attention, well, you can’t do much better than a bow.”

As if for emphasis, Tanis pulls an arrow from his back and, in one swift motion, nocks and looses it into the side of a nearby tree. A muted twang echoes as the bowstring snaps home, but you have to admit he’s right: the shot’s an order of magnitude quieter than a gunshot.
Never mind that it’s not hard to get a suppressor for a gun. You’re here to learn, not be pedantic.

“So yeah!” he grins, resting his hands on the top of his bow. “My family’s been handing down these shooting techniques for centuries. I can show you the quick and dirty rundown of the basics today, but if you wanna get really good with this,” he says, reaching over to run a finger along the curve of your bow. “It’ll take some time and dedication, you know.”
You nod.
“Cool! Yeah, alright, so come over here and let’s start talking about stance.”
Tanis waves you over, and for the next several minutes you’re treated to an expert study in planting your [pc.feet] a certain way.
Tanis certainly seems to be experienced with teaching humanoids, as he seems to know your lower body just as well as you do. He’s quickly got you standing like a shining example of an archer.

Your alien lower body seems to get in the way a bit, since you’ve got no proper feet to place. Tanis spends as much time trying to figure your body out as he does instructing you. Eventually, though, he finds a stance that he thinks will work for you, though it’s a bit awkward to hold for more than a few moments. “Gotta build up those abdominal muscles!” he declares, using a hand to straighten your back out.

Tanis is an experienced teacher, of that you have no doubt. He circles your tauric body with a serpent-like grace, using his bow like a staff to shove your legs into proper form. You suck in a sharp breath when his hand is suddenly on your back, forcing you to sit up straight as an arrow.
<i>“There we go, much better. Just hold that pose for a minute. Pretty different from just shooting a gun, right? It’s all about the stance, trust me. Well, that and a lot of hand-eye coordination.
But I think you’ve got that covered.
But we’ll work on that. No worries.

You dutifully follow Tanis’s direction, following the shirtless centaur with your eyes as he sidles up beside you and draws back another arrow. He guides you through it, correcting slight imperfections in your draw and the way you hold your arrows. All the while, he’s nothing but positive, all smiles and bright eyes even as he’s correcting the same silly mistake for the third time.

Finally, you’re ready to take your first actual shot of the session. Tanis plants a guiding hand on your shoulder, pressing in close enough that he’s practically looking down the shaft of your arrow with you. He points out a low-hanging purple fruit on a nearby tree and gives the word to bring it down.

You draw back, breathe out, and let fly. The arrow sinks into the fruit with explosive force, splattering it all over the forest floor. You pump a victorious fist in the air, and are rewarded with a slap on the back from your tauric teacher.
“Haha! Awesome work. You’re a natural, [pc.name].”

You let loose, and watch with a sinking heart as the arrow sails wide, barely nicking your target before hammering into the tree’s bark. With a sigh, you slump your shoulders and plant your bow in the dirt.
“Hey, don’t give up,” Tanis laughs, squeezing your shoulder. “That was damn close. We’ll get you there, don’t you doubt it.”
Easy for him to say.

“Alright, let’s call it here for now,” Tanis says, stretching enough to audibly crack his back. “Can’t just leave the shop abandoned all day, after all. You’ve got the basics down, I think, but we can pick it up again if you want to really dig into it. Especially if you want to use that bow of yours for self defense. Some might say that’s a suboptimal choice compared to, say, a laser pistol or a plasma caster, but a bow like this is a real tack-driver. A more precise weapon from a more noble time, you know.”

Giving you another pat on the shoulder, Tanis grabs his arrows off the ground and starts heading back inside. You follow close behind, feeling more confident about your archery skills for having trained with him.
Next
“Hey, Tanis,” you say, leaning over the leithan’s desk. “Think you could spare a few minutes to do some bow practice?”
“I dunno,” he laughs, looking around the otherwise empty office. “There’s this big line behind you. I’d hate to keep them all waiting.”
You give him a look, and he grins and laughs. “Alright, alright, if you’re gonna twist my arm. C’mon out back, and let’s set some targets up.”

He hops up from behind the desk, onto his six black-scaled legs, and grabs the compound bow he was using last time from underneath. With it, Tanis pulls out several paper shooter’s targets rolled up with a rubber band, and tucks them under his bare arm. You follow him outside once he’s collected his gear, and spend a few minutes tacking up the targets to the sides of the trees closest to the edge of the back lot.

“Cool!” he announces when you’ve got a good selection of targets up, ranging from black silhouettes to images of terrifying alien abominations attacking particularly buxom leithan girls... you’re not sure those are actually meant to be used as targets, but hey. “Alright, remember what I taught you about stance? Let’s get you standing like a marks
wo
man, and we’ll get started.”

You do as Tanis tells you, resuming the shooter’s stance he taught you during your first session. He quickly corrects a few small errors, making sure you’re standing perfectly before you bring your bow to bear on the closest target.
“Looks good. Now let’s just warm up first, get the blood flowing.”

Under Tanis’s supervision, you take several well-placed shots at the target, getting into the groove of quickly drawing, aiming, and letting fly in fluid motions. When you’ve turned the first target into a porcupine of feathered shafts, Tanis moves you onto the next, putting you through simple drills, then more complex ones, gently correcting every mistake until you’re landing every shot in the center ring.

“Gettin’ pretty badass with that bow!” Tanis laughs, giving you a friendly thump on the shoulder. “See, you don’t need fancy head’s up displays and holographic sights to be a sharpshooter. It’s all about the skill and dedication, right?”
You nod in agreement - you really feel like you’re starting to get the hang of this.
Then again, you almost wish you weren’t - the way Tanis presses his chest to your back when he adjusts your stance, the sight of his rippling muscles tensing as he draws his own bow back... yum.

“Keep practicing like this, and I might have to take you hunting sometime,” Tanis laughs, watching you plant another arrow into a tentacle-monster target. “See how you do against some of the real monsters back on Leitha.
I think you’d do pretty well.
Well, maybe with a little more experience, anyway.

The two of you do a few more short drills, but fatigue is starting to set in, slowing your movements until Tanis calls for a break. “Alright, I think that’s enough for today. Good job, [pc.name]. You’re really improving!”
You give him a grin and sling your bow over your back, following the towering ‘taur back into the scout office.
You can’t help but look at that big, muscular ass all the way in.
Next
“Still think you could learn a thing or two from me?” Tanis says, recognizing the familiar look in your eye. “I think you’ve got the basics pretty much nailed down, you know.
You’re a pretty accomplished hunter on your own, I bet.
If you really want to improve, you’ve got to go out there and put these skills to the test.

After a moment’s thought, though, Tanis chuckles and says “Fine, fine, if only for the camaraderie of it. I’ve enjoyed our training sessions, you know. It’s been more fun than I’d have thought, passing the family tradition on.”

“So let’s go,” you say, nodding towards to the door. Tanis grins and grabs his bow, along with several targets, and leads you out into the back lot. The two of you go through the usual drill, tacking targets to trees and stretching out your muscles with a few practice shots.

When the warm-up’s done, though, Tanis doesn’t start you on the same drills as your first few sessions. Instead, the towering leithan hunter rolls his shoulders and pulls a strange arrow off of his back, tipped with something blinking and clearly electronic. You cock an eyebrow and, at Tanis’s beckoning, step back. He flashes you a grin, nocks the arrow, and lets fly at a tree near the back of the lot.
The arrow slams home, quivering audibly as it sticks into the bark of the tree. A moment passes, and then a deafening roar blasts through the woods, kicking up leaves and grass in a explosive bubble.

You cover your eyes, gasping as the shockwave rolls over you. The first thing you hear, once you’re able to again, is Tanis laughing hysterically. “I guess I should have said, uh, ‘fire in the hole’ or something, right?”

“Trick arrows are the key to keeping bow hunting relevant today,” he says. “I made these myself - concussive arrows. Sends a shockwave that’ll stun just about anything, even most robots. Give you time to plunge a few more arrows, or a good blade, into whatever’s trying to fight you. Here, take a few to test out. I’ll show you how to make ‘em later.”
A quiver of arrows is shoved into your hand, and you strap them on.
“You’ve got your own, I presume?” he says, brushing leaves off his bare shoulders. “I did show you how to make ‘em, right?”

You draw one of your special leithan arrows and nock it, aiming at the same tree Tanis just shot - it looks completely unharmed, for all the force just brought against it. You aim and let fly, sending another beeping arrow to slam home into the gray bark. It detonates a moment later, blasting the targets you’d tacked up off the trees - and setting off a cacophony of howling animals, screeching birds, and a car alarm from inside the shop’s lot.

“Whew, that was loaded a little heavy!” Tanis laughs, clapping you on the shoulder, almost hard enough to stagger you. “Good shot, though. Now, the trick is to stun your target, then use the opportunity to hit it as many times as possible while it’s staggered - or to get the hell away, if you’re in a bad spot. Opening with a concussion shot can save your life if you’re ambushed while wounded or exhausted. So, let’s see you do some quick follow-up shots.”

You give Tanis a nod, and let fly with a concussive arrow, blasting one of the few remaining targets left standing. The moment the arrow detonates, you draw one of your mundane arrows, nock, and let fly as close to the first point of impact as you can. In what feels like the span of a heartbeat, you hammer three more arrows where the first hand landed.
“Quick as a cunt-snake,” Tanis says as you lower your bow. “I think you’ve got the idea. Now let’s drive it home, [pc.name]...”

The rest of your session is spent in quick-shot drills and testing out different mixes of concussive arrows. Tanis works you hard - harder than he ever has before - leaving you panting and sweaty by the time he finally calls it. Your muscles ache from drawing your bow so many times... but it’s a good pain, one you earned through hard work, and growing skill. Tanis smiles at you, an arm around your shoulder to guide you back inside.
“Let’s take a break... and maybe get you a shower,” he chuckles. “Good work out there, [pc.name]. You’re getting damn good with a bow
- maybe there’s a little leithan in you!
. I think the leithan part of you’s really taking off!
Man, it feels good to be passing along the family skills. Maybe I ought to open an archery school some time.
After you get inside, Tanis grabs a data slate off his desk. “Oh, hey, hold up a second [pc.name]. Lemme give you something. What’s your e-mail address?”

You tell it to him, and after he hits a few keys on his pad, your Codex beeps out that it’s received a new message. “Cool. So, those are the plans to make concussive arrows like the ones we were just using. If you’ve got some technical expertise, or a couple hours to dick around on the extranet, you should be able to fix some up for yourself.”
“Wow, thanks,” you say, pocketing your Codex after a quick look. The plans seem simple enough.
He grins. “Any time, man. Let me know how they work out for you!”

You approach the local Scout Authority representative and awkwardly ask him if he would happen to know someone who can make taur-specific installations on a ship.
“Say what, [pc.sir]?” he asks with a raised eyebrow.

“Well, there’s someone I’d like to invite to move in with me, but she’s a deertaur with, uhh, special needs. And my ship doesn’t have the necessary ‘equipment’ to keep her satisfied,” you elaborate, hoping he’ll catch your meaning.
As the leithan listens to your story, a knowing smirk forms on his lips. “Oh, the cute deertaur that roams the jungle. I’ve seen her a couple of times.”

Considering how horny she always is, you find it hard not to imagine her making passes at someone as hunky as Tanis. So, before the topic can derail into TMI, you explain that, since Tanis has a tauric body too, you thought maybe he might know someone who could provide that sort of equipment.
“Well, aren’t you a lucky one? I just happen to have some I’m not using.”
“That sounds convenient,” you say skeptically.

He shrugs. “Ever since I took this job, I haven’t really needed it anymore. The locals see my ‘size’ and are more than happy to lend a hand, if you catch my drift. It’s just collecting dust in my room, so I could be convinced to part with it.”
“How much?”

The leithan cups his chin in thought for a moment. “I’m guessing I would have to carry it to your ship and install it myself, since no-one else ‘round these parts would know how. Does ".concat(e.toLocaleString()," credits sound good?”")

original: When Tanis asks where you want to go, you tell him that you’re not looking to peruse his taxi services at present. Rather, you’d like to ask a favor of him.
His cute ears perk up at that. “If it’s something I can do, sure!”
You ask him if he remembers Amber, the deertaur that used to roam the jungle.
“Oh, how could I forget...” the scout says with a dreamy look in his eyes, his lips curling into a smirk. It would seem he’s helped Amber with her ‘special needs’ before.

Well, this should make things fast. You explain to him that you’re looking into a cure for her little problem, but for that you require gene samples from tauric species that have an actual heat cycle. Leithans fit the bill to a T.
He cocks his head to the side, confused. “I’m not sure why you’d want to do that, [pc.mr] Steele, but I suppose it doesn’t hurt to help after my shift is over. Do I need to go to a lab, or...”
“That won’t be required, we can collect it right now.” Using the gene extraction kit given by Lumi, you easily take a blood sample from the leithan. He doesn’t even feel the pinprick.
“That’s it?” he asks, almost disappointed.
[pc.isAss|You nod. ]“Thank you for your cooperation, Tanis[pc.isAss|.”|,” you say cheerfully.] As you make to leave, however, he stops you.

“[pc.Sir], wait, I uhhh... Please forgive my impertinence, but please make sure to take good care of her, will you? I didn’t get a chance to talk with the girl all that much, but I got the sense that she’s been through a lot,” he says with a surprisingly somber tone.

You [pc.nmh|give the scout a reassuring smile and tell him she’s in good hands. You wouldn’t be going through all this trouble if you didn’t care about her.|raise an eyebrow and tell him you wouldn’t be going through all this trouble if you didn’t care about her.|scoff and tell the scout not to worry his pretty head. She’s in good hands.]
He smiles [pc.isNice|back|with relief]. “Alright, [pc.mr] Steele, I’m glad she’s found someone like you.”

Esbeth’s scout is a full-blooded leithan, measuring almost 10 ft (3 meters) in height and sporting a rather athletic physique, albeit on the slender side. Elfin ears jut out at the sides of his head, complemented by a pair of long, antennae-like ears covered in dark, chitinous scales that sprout right above his brow. His face itself is quite attractive by human standards, with a fairly masculine profile and just a hint of androgyny. Sky-blue eyes and golden-blond hair complete the handsome package.
	
	The lizard-taur’s humanoid half has light-gray skin and a fairly standard pair of arms, while the tauric half is covered in darker scales and has six reptilian legs, capped with yellow claws. A thick, lizard-like tail drags behind him, though it appears to be more dexterous than one might expect, judging from how it deftly avoids random objects in the cluttered office.
	
	Like natural armor, large plates of glossy, chitin-like material provide an additional layer of protection to much of his lower body, as well as his tail, hands, forearms and the sides of his human torso — the latter plates appearing almost like a lewd half-corset, if you choose to look at it that way.
	
	Bioluminescent yellow light shines faintly through the gaps between his plates, giving the leithan an even more offworldly look, but you’d be hard-pressed to deny that it’s[pc.mfn
		| cool as fuck
		| strangely alluring
		| strangely alluring
	]. Like any leithan, the exterior chitin-like armor can glow as a whole when he experiences strong emotions — or extreme sexual ecstasy.
